data.title: Maitong Sunshine Cultural Development Co., Ltd SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Maitong Sunshine Cultural Development Co., Ltd, a company primarily engaged in the sale of cultural and creative products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the fourth quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, largely due to an economic slowdown in China and a strategic shift in business focus.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $24,905, a significant decrease of 98% compared to $1,022,155 in the same period last year, primarily due to reduced consumer spending and strategic focus on existing members.
  • Gross Profit: $12,099, reflecting a gross profit margin of 49% for the period, down from $379,808 in the previous year.
  • Income from operations: $(120,745), a decline from $273,893 in the prior year, driven by increased operating expenses and reduced revenue.
  • Net income / (loss): $(120,601), compared to a net income of $166,993 in the previous year, impacted by lower sales and higher operating costs.
  • Basic and diluted earnings per share: $(0.0020), a decrease from $0.0028 in the same period last year, reflecting the overall decline in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: During the three months ended December 31, 2025, all revenue was generated by the subsidiary Tongzhilian, which engaged solely in product sales. This marks a shift from the previous year, where 80% of revenue was also from product sales, indicating a continued focus on this segment.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily based in China, with all revenues generated from customers in the People's Republic of China. The economic slowdown in China has significantly impacted consumer spending, particularly in the mid-to-high-end customized travel segment.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to diversify its product offerings by introducing more affordable travel packages and expanding collaborations within the travel ecosystem. Additionally, there is an emphasis on enhancing employee training to improve service quality and customer satisfaction.
  • Operational Strategy: The company is prioritizing service quality and relationship-building with existing members over aggressive new customer acquisition, aiming to foster long-term loyalty and sustainable recurring revenue.
  • Market Challenges: The company faced significant challenges due to weakening macroeconomic conditions in China and heightened consumer pessimism, which led to a 98% reduction in revenue compared to the previous year.

SEC Filing: Maitong Sunshine Cultural Development Co., Ltd [ MGSD ] - 10-Q - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Sensex Closes Slightly Higher
data.description:
India's BSE Sensex finished about 0.4% up at 82,814.7 after a choppy session on Friday, partly reflecting a technical bounce following the sharp sell-off in the previous day.

However, sentiment stayed cautious amid ongoing US-Iran tensions, worries about AI disruptions and uncertainty over the Federal Reserve’s rate-cut path.

On the data front, flash PMI data showed India's private sector activity accelerated to a three-month high in February, driven primarily by strong manufacturing output.

data.title: Will US Strike Iran Anytime Soon Given Trump's 10-Day Window For More Clarity? Here's What Bettors Think Amid The Military Build-Up In Middle East
data.description:
Key points:
  • According to a Bloomberg report, President Trump told reporters aboard Air Force One on Thursday that 10 to 15 days was “pretty much” the maximum amount of time he would give for negotiations for a nuclear deal with Iran.
  • Despite the burgeoning U.S. military buildup in the Middle East, bettors on Polymarket think that the odds of a strike on Iran over the next month are 50:50 at most.
  • During an address at a Board of Peace event on Thursday, President Trump said that the next 10 days will show whether the U.S.-Iran tussle escalates or a deal is struck.

Prediction markets are buzzing with activity over the prospect of a U.S. strike on Iran after President Donald Trump gave the Middle Eastern country 10 to 15 days to strike a nuclear deal.

According to a Bloomberg report, President Trump told reporters aboard Air Force One on Thursday that 10 to 15 days was “pretty much” the maximum amount of time he would give for negotiations for a nuclear deal with Iran.

“We’re either going to get a deal, or it’s going to be unfortunate for them,” he added.

Amid this, the U.S. military has deployed a vast array of forces in the Middle East, comprising fighter jets, refueling tankers, and two aircraft carriers, according to the report.

What Are Prediction Markets Indicating?

Despite the burgeoning U.S. military buildup in the Middle East, bettors on Polymarket think that the odds of a strike on Iran over the next month are 50:50 at most.

At the time of writing, the odds of a strike on Iran between February 20 and March 7 were in the range of 2% to 42%. March 15 is the first time these odds reach 50%. Thereafter, the odds continue to climb, reaching 76% by the end of the year.

Bettors on Kalshi are pessimistic about the U.S. and Iran striking a nuclear deal, which would avert a strike.

The odds of a deal being struck before April plummeted 39 percentage points over the past 24 hours, falling to 27%. The chances of a deal before August 2026 are even lower, at just 16%. Bettors are slightly more optimistic that the deal will be reached before 2027, at 48%.

The total betting volumes on Polymarket regarding a U.S. strike on Iran stood at a staggering $323 million, while Kalshi users placed bets worth $189,000 about the odds of a nuclear deal.

Trump’s Warning To Iran

During an address at a Board of Peace event on Thursday, President Trump said that the next 10 days will show whether the U.S.-Iran tussle escalates or a deal is struck.

“Now we may have to take it a step further, or we may not. Maybe we're going to make a deal. You're going to be finding out over the next probably 10 days,” he said.

Rafael Mariano Grossi, Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency, also warned on Thursday that the window for Iran to strike a nuclear deal is at risk of closing.

“There is not much time, but we are working on something concrete. There are a couple of solutions the IAEA has proposed,” Grossi said.

Meanwhile, U.S. equities were mixed in Friday’s pre-market trade. At the time of writing, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY), which tracks the S&P 500 index, edged lower by 0.01%, the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) rose 0.03%, while the SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (DIA) declined 0.07%. Retail sentiment around the S&P 500 ETF on Stocktwits was in the ‘bearish’ territory.

The iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (TLT) was up by 0.17% at the time of writing, while the iShares 7-10 Year Treasury Bond ETF (IEF) rose 0.02%.

data.title: AngloGold Ashanti 2025 free cash flow triples to $2.9 bln on strong production growth
data.description:
Overview

  • Gold miner's 2025 free cash flow tripled to $2.9 bln, driven by production growth

  • Adjusted EBITDA more than doubled to $6.3 bln in 2025

  • Company declared total 2025 dividends of $1.8 bln, highest ever

Outlook

  • AngloGold Ashanti forecasts 2026 group gold production between 2.80Moz and 3.17Moz

  • Company expects 2026 total cash costs between $1,315/oz and $1,430/oz

  • AngloGold Ashanti forecasts 2026 AISC between $1,780/oz and $1,990/oz

Result Drivers

  • GOLD PRODUCTION INCREASE - Group Gold production rose 16% y-o-y to 3.1Moz, driven by full-year contribution from Sukari and improved performance at certain assets

  • HIGHER GOLD PRICES - Average gold price received per ounce rose 45% y-o-y to $3,468/oz, boosting revenues and cash flow

  • PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION - Acquisition of Centamin and strategic divestitures improved focus on core assets

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

FY Adjusted EBITDA

$6.30 bln

FY Capex

$1.60 bln

FY Free Cash Flow

$2.90 bln

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 6 "strong buy" or "buy", 1 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the gold peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Anglogold Ashanti PLC is $113.50, about 5.5% above its February 19 closing price of $107.63

  • The stock recently traded at 12 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 9 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: Indonesia fines company, individuals for alleged stock manipulation, regulator says
data.description:
Indonesia has fined one company and three individuals a total of 11.05 billion rupiah ($655,000) for alleged stock market manipulation between 2016 to 2022, its chief stock market supervisor said on Friday.

Index provider MSCI in January flagged transparency issues on the Indonesian stock exchange, triggering a market rout. Jakarta was forced to tighten supervision over its capital markets and announced sweeping reform proposals aimed at restoring the confidence of foreign investors.

The MSCI warning, however, has brought to light what brokers in Indonesia have dubbed "goreng-goreng saham", or "stock frying", aimed at pumping up share prices.

Hasan Fawzi, interim chief capital market supervisor at the Financial Services Authority (OJK), told a press conference that the regulator has fined a company and two individuals for allegedly controlling multiple stock investor accounts in order to pump up share prices in a listed firm.

Another person, a social media personality identified only by their initial BVN, was fined for influencing followers into buying certain stocks, as well as using multiple accounts to conduct transactions.

Earlier this month, OJK penalised several companies for inappropriate conduct, including freezing the underwriting permit of UOB Kay Hian Sekuritas for a year for improper due diligence over clients ahead of a 2019 initial public offering.

($1 = 16,860 rupiah)

data.title: Latvia Producer Prices Rise in January
data.description:
Producer prices in Latvia rose by 1.2% year-on-year in January 2026 from a downwardly revised 0.7% growth in the previous month.

Output increased in mining and quarrying (17.4% vs 17% in December 2025).

In addition, activity recovered for electricity, gas, steam, and air-conditioning supply (1% vs -3.3%).

Meanwhile, output growth slowed in water supply, sewerage, waste management, and remediation activities (5.2% vs 5.4%) and manufacturing (0.5% vs 0.9%), with notable decreases in food products (-0.7% vs 0.3%), beverages (3.2% vs 6.1%), paper and paper products (0.3% vs 2.6%), and chemical and chemical products (1.1% vs 1.8%).

On a monthly basis, output increased by 0.6% in January, rebounding from an upwardly revised 1.1% decline in the preceding period.

data.title: LUG: Record production, strong cash flow, and major reserve growth drive robust dividends and outlook
data.description:
Record gold production and financial results in 2025, with net income up 86% year-over-year and $1.0 billion in cash flow from operations. Significant reserve additions, robust exploration results, and a strong dividend payout highlight continued growth and shareholder returns.

data.title: Euro zone bond yields at multi-month lows as investors track ECB chatter, geopolitics
data.description:
Key points:
  • Lagarde plans to complete ECB term amid succession speculation
  • German 10-year yields decline for second week
  • Euro area bonds outperform US Treasuries

By Amanda Cooper and Stefano Rebaudo

Euro zone government bond yields headed for a second straight weekly decline on Friday, in a week marked by speculation over the leadership of the European Central Bank and by rising geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

ECB President Christine Lagarde told the Wall Street Journal on Thursday she expected to complete her eight-year term at the head of the central bank, following a report earlier in the week she was planning to step down.

That story, reported by the Financial Times on Wednesday, added impetus to the race to succeed Lagarde, bringing potential uncertainty to the ECB, which markets expect to hold interest rates at 2% for an extended period.

At the same time, investors are keeping a close eye on the Middle East, where a massive U.S. military buildup is fuelling fears of a wider war. President Donald Trump on Thursday warned Iran it must make a deal over its nuclear programme or "really bad things" will happen, while Tehran has threatened to retaliate against U.S. bases in the region if attacked, triggering a rise in the price of crude this week.

German 10-year yields (DE10YT=RR) were set for a decline of 2.5 basis points this week, which on the back of last week's 9-bp drop, brings the fall for February so far to more than 11 bps, the most since April last year.

The 10-year Bunds, a reference point for the wider euro zone market, were last yielding 2.73%, down around 1 bp on the day and just above this week's 2-1/2-month low of 2.725%.

Euro zone business activity accelerated faster than forecast this month.

EURO AREA BONDS OUTPACE US TREASURIES

Some analysts said Bunds have outperformed U.S. Treasuries in recent sessions, reflecting a view that German debt offers greater safety in the event of Iran‑related turmoil.

Yields on Italian BTPs and other euro zone bonds, including those of France and Spain are also hovering around multi‑month lows, having outpaced Treasuries too.

The benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yield traded at 4.07%, up from 4.018% hit earlier this week, the lowest level since November 28.

In the U.S., investors will get a first read of fourth-quarter gross domestic product, which is forecast to have expanded by 3%, and of the Federal Reserve's preferred measure of inflation, the core personal consumption expenditures price index.

Commerzbank strategists said they believed Bunds could come under some pressure, taking their cue from a touch of weakness in the U.S. Treasury market, after minutes from the Fed's last meeting showed policymakers were divided over the outlook for monetary policy, given the persistence of inflation.

"Everything considered, we suggest selling Bunds into strength this morning," Christoph Rieger, who is chief rates strategist at Commerzbank, said.

Italian 10-year yields were down 2 bps on the day at 3.344%, their lowest in four months, while French 10-year debt yields were also down nearly 1.5 bps at 3.302%, their lowest in over six months.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Rises. It's Nearing $30 Billion OpenAI Investment. Report Says. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Alex Kozul-Wright

Nvidia is reportedly close to agreeing a $30 billion investment into ChatGPT-developer OpenAI.

Nvidia shares were up 0.4% to $186.66 in premarket trading Friday. The stock has risen 2.8% over the past five trading sessions.

Among other chip makers, Advanced Micro Devices was up 0.3% while Broadcom rose 0.6% in premarket trading.

Nvidia, the leading maker of artificial-intelligence chips, is close to agreeing to invest $30 billion into OpenAI, according to the Financial Times, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

It would replace a commitment, announced in September, in which Nvidia said it would invest up to $100 billion in OpenAI to support the buildout of its AI data center capacity, according to the FT.

OpenAI didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Barron's early on Friday. Nvidia declined to comment.

OpenAI is reportedly seeking $100 billion in new funding, which would value the ChatGPT-maker at roughly $830 billion. Much of the money raised would likely be spent on AI chips from Nvidia and its peers.

OpenAI is preparing for a public listing as soon as the fourth quarter of this year, according to The Wall Street Journal. A successful IPO of OpenAI would likely lift all AI-exposed stocks, including Nvidia.

Write to Alex Kozul-Wright at alexander.kozul-wright@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AITX's RAD Books Three More RIO Mini Units, Client Deployment Rises to Eight
data.description:
AITX announced RAD secured three additional RIO Mini units, bringing the client's total deployment to eight systems licensed with SARA.

Key Highlights:

  • RAD sold three additional RIO Mini units to a major consumer products manufacturer; installation scheduled in coming weeks.
  • The repeat order raises this client's total deployment to eight RIO Mini systems across multiple facilities, signaling rollout confidence.
  • Each new unit is licensed with SARA, increasing attachment rate and strengthening recurring revenue visibility.
  • Company completed SOC 2 Type 2 audit, validating internal controls and reinforcing credibility with enterprise and government clients.

Original SEC Filing: Artificial Intelligence Technology Solutions Inc. [ AITX ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
